channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Syncing my notes folder between laptop and phone
item.description: I wanted to sync my notes folder that was on my laptop with a folder on my phone, so I could access and edit it in both places. For this I tried 2 approaches: syncing with rclone on laptop and...

item.title: Making Sure Your Prompt Will Be There For You When You Need It
item.description: At Google, our team (Google Cloud Samples) uses Gemini to produce thousands of samples in batches. In doing so, we've learned that the biggest hurdle isn't the AI, it's our own expectations about...

item.title: nono : the ultimate coding agent security tool - sandbox and supercharge Claude Code in just two simple steps!
item.description: Claude Code is one of the most capable coding agents available today. It can navigate your codebase, edit files across multiple directories, run shell commands, install packages, and execute tests...

item.title: The true hidden cost of slow and inefficient PR reviews
item.description: Every growing engineering team, at some point, hits a limit that has nothing to do with code quality or test coverage. It’s the PR review bottleneck. The code is ready, tests are passing, CI isn’t...

item.title: Understanding Go Interfaces with a Practical Example
item.description: One of the most powerful features in Go is interfaces. They allow different types to share behaviour without requiring inheritance or explicit declarations. If you're coming from languages like...

item.title: The Advanced Cybersecurity Ego Taxonomy
item.description: For seasoned observers who can identify species by posture alone. 1. The Drift-Blind Executive Habitat: Boardrooms, earnings calls, "strategic offsites." Call: "We trust our people." Treats...

item.title: Why I Built a Screen Time Tracker That Never Sees Your Data
item.description: I spend too much time on my phone. This is not a confession, it's a demographic fact. The average is somewhere around 3 hours a day, and I was right in that range. Mostly Instagram Reels, mostly...

item.title: Part 1: Serverless Is Not a Silver Bullet. What Lambdas Are Actually For
item.description: There's a particular kind of technical mistake that's easy to make and expensive to fix. It doesn't come from ignorance, exactly. It comes from taking something that works well in one context and...

item.title: Been using cmux daily for running AI agents — loved it so much I sent 3 PRs. Localized Japanese U...
item.description: Been using cmux daily for running AI agents — loved it so much I sent 3 PRs. Localized Japanese UI, fixed an omnibar hang, then tracked down a CJK font bug where decorative fonts got picked over...

item.title: I added ViewComponent & Shared Partial support to 52 Rails UI components (Rails Blocks Update)
item.description: Hi, I'm Alex and I created Rails Blocks, a UI component library for Rails apps that I started last summer. Over the last few weeks, I reworked the docs for all 52 component sets to support 3...

item.title: Fake AI Content About the Iran War Is All Over X
item.description: X’s Grok is failing to accurately verify video footage from the Iran conflict and is sharing its own AI-generated images about the war.

item.title: AgentMail raises $6M to build an email service for AI agents
item.description: AgentMail provides an API platform that lets you give AI agents their own email inboxes, with support for two-way conversations, parsing, threading, labeling, searching, and replying.

item.title: Satechi’s new folding dock adds USB, audio, and video ports to the iPad
item.description: Satechi has announced a redesigned version of the iPad Pro stand with USB-C hub functionality it launched nearly five years ago that's smaller and easier to travel with. The new OntheGo Foldable...

item.title: KadNap Malware Infects 14,000+ Edge Devices to Power Stealth Proxy Botnet
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malware called KadNap that's primarily targeting Asus routers to enlist them into a botnet for proxying malicious traffic. The malware, first...

item.title: Grammarly will keep using authors’ identities without permission unless they opt out
item.description: Last week, my colleagues discovered that Superhuman's Grammarly had turned me into an AI editor, using my real name, without ever asking my permission. They did the same to my boss Nilay Patel, my...

item.title: The best Mario Day deals we found
item.description: It’s Mario Day, an annual celebration of Nintendo’s mascot. It falls every year on March 10th because the date spells out “MAR10,” but you probably knew that already. Even if you aren’t all that...

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 4.5 with 6x Frame Generation is rolling out at the end of March
item.description: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 with 6x Multi Frame Generation will be available starting March 31st for users with RTX 50-series GPUs, the company announced on Tuesday. With 6x Multi Frame Generation, Nvidia...

item.title: Every influencer eventually becomes a merch store
item.description: Tucker Carlson's online store sells right-wing apparel and home goods, like hoodies in the Supreme streetwear style mocking Somali people or mugs with The Godfather puppetmaster iconography edited...

item.title: The gym-friendly Beats Powerbeats Pro 2 are 20 percent off right now
item.description: Beats may have recently redesigned the Beats Fit Pro to be more comfortable and secure, but the company’s latest offering is still no match for its other pair of fitness-centric earbuds: the...

item.title: Meta acquires Moltbook, the Reddit-like network for AI agents
item.description: Meta is acquiring Moltbook, a Reddit-like platform where AI agents can make and comment on posts, as first reported by Axios. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Matthew Tye confirmed...

item.title: Shark’s ChillPill fan can cool your skin like an ice pack
item.description: SharkNinja has announced a new personal cooling device called the Shark ChillPill that takes three approaches to beating the heat. Unlike other wearable cooling devices that simply rely on moving...

item.title: Thinking Machines Lab inks massive compute deal with Nvidia
item.description: The multi-year deal involves at least a gigawatt of compute power and also includes a strategic investment from Nvidia.

item.title: ‘Age Verification’ could force trans people to out themselves to use the internet
item.description: In 2026, a photo ID is not just paperwork - it essentially grants you permission to exist in society. Last month, Kansas legislature passed a law categorically invalidating trans people's driver's...

item.title: Google gives in to users’ complaints over AI-powered ‘Ask Photos’ search feature
item.description: The option appears on the Google Photos Search screen and lets users pick which experience they want.

item.title: Legora reaches $5.55 billion valuation as AI legal tech boom endures
item.description: Legora, an AI platform for lawyers, is now valued at $5.55 billion following a $550 million Series D led by Accel to fuel its growth in the U.S.

item.title: Meta acquired Moltbook, the AI agent social network that went viral because of fake posts
item.description: Meta says that Moltbook's approach to "connecting agents through an always-on-directory" is novel.

item.title: I reviewed the MacBook Neo, ask me anything
item.description: It's MacBook Neo review day, so let's talk Apple's new $599 laptop. I'm going to host another AMA for Verge subscribers today at 11AM PT / 2PM ET. Like last time, it'll all take place in the...

item.title: YouTube expands AI deepfake detection to politicians, government officials, and journalists
item.description: YouTube's AI deepfake detection tool is becoming available to politicians, journalists, and officials, letting them flag unauthorized likenesses for removal.

item.title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
item.description: Pokémon Go was the world’s first augmented-reality megahit. Released in 2016 by the Google spinout Niantic, the AR twist on the juggernaut Pokémon franchise fast became a global phenomenon. From...

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and...
